name: Baked Pennette with Vegetables and Cheese (Mandel)
recipeIngredient: 10 ounces pennette, or other short pasta
5 cups diced vegetables, 3/4-inch assortment -- such as mushrooms; zucchini; eggplant OR roasted peppers
3 cups shredded skim mozzarella OR monterey jack cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook pasta in plenty of boiling salted water until tender but still firm, 10 to 12 minutes, or according to package instructions.
2. Drain and toss with 1 tablespoon olive oil.
3. Heat remaining 1 tablespoon oil in a 12-inch nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. When hot, add vegetables and fennel seeds. Sprinkle with 1/2 teaspoon salt and stir-fry just until vegetables are barely tender, about 3 minutes.
4. Stir in red pepper flakes to taste, garlic, and pasta sauce. Taste and adjust seasoning; mixture should be highly seasoned.
5. Transfer mixture to a large bowl. Add pasta and toss well.
6. Layer half of the pasta in a shallow, greased 2 1/2-quart casserole. Sprinkle with 1 1/2 cups mozzarella.
7. Top with remaining pasta and sprinkle with remaining mozzarella.
8. Bake at 350 degrees, tented loosely with foil, for 35 minutes. Remove foil and bake until sizzling and edges of cheese are browned, 10 to 15 minutes. Let rest 10 minutes before serving. Pass Parmesan cheese separately.
[6 servings. Each serving without Parmesan cheese: 362 calories; 463 mg sodium; 15 mg cholesterol; 10 grams fat; 49 grams carbohydrates; 17 grams protein; 2.77 grams fiber.]
